front: Composer: American minimalist; operas include Nixon in China, The Death of Klinghoffer, and Doctor Atomic; On the Transmigration of Souls won the 2003 Pulitzer
Composer: frequent collaborator with theater director Peter Sellars, who directed and wrote libretti for Nixon in China, The Death of Klinghoffer, Doctor Atomic, and Girls of the Golden West
Opera: depicts Nixon's 1972 visit to China; opens with the Spirit of '76 landing; Nixon attends The Red Detachment of Women; preceded by The Chairman Dances
Opera: libretto by Alice Goodman; Pat Nixon sings 'This is prophetic' in the Summer Palace; aria 'News Has a Kind of Mystery' is sung after a jet lands in Beijing
Opera: about the Achille Lauro hijacking; the protagonist sings 'Aria of the Falling Body' as he is thrown overboard; a character is shot by the hijacker Molqi
Opera: about J. Robert Oppenheimer; Oppenheimer sings 'Batter my heart, three-person'd God'; uses a Tewa song and the Bhagavad Gita; libretto by Peter Sellars
Oratorio: 9/11 memorial; tape reads names of the dead and plays city sounds; a boy's voice repeats 'missing' in a heartbeat rhythm; won the 2003 Pulitzer Prize
Overture: 'fanfare for orchestra' for Pittsburgh Symphony (1986); opens with a woodblock ostinato; inspired by the feeling of riding in a fast sports car then regretting it
back: Short Ride in a Fast Machine (Adams)
work: Short Ride in a Fast Machine

front: Orchestral work: Adams depicted dreaming of an oil tanker lifting off from water and two limousines turning into pianos
String work: inner movements 'Hymning Slews' and 'Loops and Verses'; reminds Adams of the energetic dancing of the Shakers
Piano work: begins in the Lydian mode of A; oscillates between two modes while repeatedly modulating around the circle of fifths
